sourceMovie.title: Smurfs
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Smurfs (2025) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Chris Miller's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Aladdin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Smurfs clicked for you, Aladdin (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Smurfs clicked for you, The Super Mario Bros. Movie (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Smurfs?
faq.answer: Try Aladdin, The Super Mario Bros. Movie, The Nightmare Before Christmas—each shares DNA with Smurfs while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Smurfs?
faq.answer: Smurfs (2025) is best described as Animation, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Smurfs worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
